notes: KNOWN_FAILING (daemon=bug). Spec asserts the CORRECT contract: the user cancels a long in-flight turn with ESC and the session goes ready→working→ready, NOT sticking in working post-interrupt, then a recovery prompt drives a clean ready→working→ready arc proving the parser reset. WHAT GEMINI WRITES ON ESC (v0.46.0): handleUserCancelledEvent flushes any pending item then appends addItem({type:"info",text:"User cancelled the request."}); the useHistoryManager chat-recording switch records info/error/warning notices via chatRecordingService.recordMessage but BREAKS for user/gemini, and processGeminiStreamEvents returns UserCancelled→the caller early-returns before the post-stream gemini recordMessage. So exactly one type:"info" MessageRecord lands on disk for the cancelled turn and NO terminal gemini message does. WHY KNOWN_FAILING: the gemini-cli parser dispatches strictly on type (user/gemini handled, default incl. info/error/warning → Skip; parser.go:100-109,63-65) and infers turn_done only from a final text-only gemini message (parser.go:208-212). So the cancel notice is dropped, no turn_done fires, IsAgentDone() never trips, classifier rule 3 cannot fire (the parser never sets IsUserInterrupt), and with no inactivity sweep (stale-tool timer deleted PR #106) the session STICKS in working until the recovery turn's terminal gemini message lands. The live recording is expected to show the interrupt_to_ready phase FAIL (state stuck in working) and the recovery arc settle it; do not weaken the spec to match the bug. The fix is local (map the cancel type:"info" notice — or info/error/warning notices generally — to a terminal signal in the gemini parser), the same shape as turn-aborted-by-error. Recorded via the INTERACTIVE driver (gemini -y under tmux: send, sleep, interrupt, sleep, recovery send, wait_turn, trailing sleep) so the process outlives the observation window; the `interrupt` step must be ported from the reference driver first (driver=gap:interrupt). The phase shape mirrors turn-aborted-by-error/basic-turn because the visible contract is the same arc.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch
Long-essay prompt drives ready → working before the ESC interrupt

ESC cancels the in-flight turn; the cancel notice (type:"info" 'User cancelled the request.') should drive working → ready — state must NOT stick in working post-interrupt (KNOWN_FAILING today: parser skips type:"info" → no turn_done → stays working)
Recovery prompt drives ready → working, proving the parser reset after the interrupt
Recovery turn completes back to ready; clean ready → working → ready arc, never entered waiting
